A twilight which has the lustre of blue lily, beryl or lotus-
filaments, which is free from strong winds and which is brightened
by the Sun's rays, produces rain the same day.

290 Brhaf SamhitH
Smfa STF^Vt'TVTt ll^?ll
A twilight which possesses clouds of inauspicious forms (like
those of an ass, a camel, a headless trunk, a crow, a cat etc.), an
aerial city, fog, smoke and dust, produces drought in the rainy
season, and clash of arms in other seasons.

5T^n f^ffrTT^n
In the six seasons beginning with Si sir a or winter the natural
colours of the sky at twilight are in order red, yellow, white, varie-
gated, lotus-coloured (red and white) and crimson (blood-red).
Each colour at twilight is beneficial in its own season, and harmful
if it is contrary.
